key_points: Lushkoff used to be a singer in a Russian choir
He became a beggar after losing his job due to alcoholism
Sergei exposes his lies and encourages him to work
Olga secretly chops the wood for him out of compassion
Lushkoff later reforms and becomes a notary earning a stable salary
characters: Lushkoff – Represents weakness and later moral reform
Sergei – Represents discipline and moral guidance
themes: Power of compassion in human reform
literary_devices: Irony – Sergei thinks he reformed Lushkoff but Olga actually influenced him
Characterization – Chekhov develops Lushkoff’s personality gradually
exam_tips: Explain Olga’s emotional influence on Lushkoff
Mention both Sergei’s guidance and Olga’s compassion
Highlight the social issue of begging and poverty
Discuss the moral message of the story
